review_text: First time staying in Perth with the family and everything was fantastic, the staff were incredible, rooms were cozy and well maintained, beds were very comfortable, gardens and park were extremely well kept. Will definitely be back! Thankyou!

Friendly helpful staff, clean, tidy cabins, well looked after grounds, wish we knew about you sooner.
Such a hidden gem!

Stayed in a single room
Very old smelt strongly off cleaning products...won't stay again

One of our favourite places to stay whilst in Perth, we stayed in a pet friendly unit #5 this time for 2 nights was wonderful had everything you needed, reception staff Andrea is great from booking over phone to her welcoming check-in. We found king size bed just a bit too hard but again that’s just personal choice. Great couple days look forward to returning.
We had a site for caravan for a few nights on our second stay here. Nice big sites, good location with easy access to Midland and 30-40 minutes north or south of city.

Loved our stay here. Great location, tidy cabin and just what we needed. Staff were friendly and helpful. Thanks for being so accommodating our pets as this was very important to us. We will definitely recommend Banksia to our friends 😀
The most beautiful & clean rooms for a super affordable price. Late check in is easy to organise & the shower facilites are top notch.

Fantastic,  I cant praise the service enough the weekend receptionest who handeled  my check in with my need for urgency,  she instantly soothed my jaded nervous system  such a pleasant young lady,  and then with the same friendly manner accomadated my last minute desire to extend my stay a few extra nights, so very gratefull for the service with a smile.  And I commend the friendly team at reception upon check out. All smiles...
Thank you I arrived exhausted and shaken and left feeling refreshed...

We enjoyed a Great stay. We like the location as we explored from here. Great local shops and businesses to stock up and get some maintenence done on our car. Internal sites can be tight for vans, 3 good drive through sites.
Ants in room walking on floor feet black
Has a nice vibe.  Not suitable for large  caravans but check. Rooms are well maintained with AC. Nice BBQ and playground
Will stay here next time.

Joli camping très bien entretenu.
Acceuil chaleureux.
Very Very nice clean and tidy and the location is great
We stayed in the lux cabin, very nice apartment style with oven, stove and microwave so great for longer stays with family. All very modern and clean. Fantastic camp grounds very neat and tidy.

Our family of 2 adults and 1 young teen stayed 5 nites.
Place was clean
But we were stunned and highly disappointed and annoyed to be told when asked if we could have another toilet roll only to be told by staff SORRY we only supply 1 unless you stay more than a week😮
How much$$ a week for a caravan

Quiet ! Nice cabin !
Wifi signal 📶 poor
Optus service: even worst

Banksia:We had a cabin, small but comfortable, in this tourist park. Adequately equipped but needs a sharp cutting knife. Communal kitchen very clean. Gardens and walkways clean and well maintained. Far from shops etc. but a bus stop outside the gate.

Banksia:
We had a cabin, small but a comfortable 3 nights, in this tourist park. Adequately equipped but needs a sharp cutting knife. Communal kitchen very clean. Gardens and walkways clean and well maintained. Far from shops etc. but a bus stop outside the gate.
A nice little place away from the hustle and bustle but close enough to reach

Our unit was very spacious and clean, the surrounding grounds and gardens were beautifully maintained. Checkin was no hassle, really good service.  The location suited us well, the distance to the airport was good!
Only drawback was not being able to enjoy the deck each evening due to a cloud of cigarette smoke from visitors in both neighbouring units.
The pool was clean and a relaxing space.
Great camp-site to stay for a couple of days or even a week...
Very unfriendly reception staff
Hmm, receptionists do not represent this business well. Very rude and cannot hold a conversation. Called twice, two different women same poor result. You lost me at hello.
Worst receptionists in history! Save your business and get rid of your poor reception staff who are the face and voice of your business.
The reception staff are completely useless!!!! As a result we could not make a booking and won't be! I have never had such poor service in my life. Two receptionists with the same outcome!

what a joke this park is, everyone's up ya, dont make a wrong move...you fill in ya got two dogs but go to the check in they say only one dog even though the policy doesn't say anything about a maximum amount of dogs, lots of permanent ones here as well, pretty annoying. if your dog only barks once and you're not near by they call ya straight away, can leave the dogs outside and go away, what a joke. shower cabins are too small to shower with ya baby without dropping it as well as changing its close in there. dog friendly my butt...never again!!!
Family friendly caravan park.
Vorletzte Übernachtung mit Wohnmobil, auf sehr ruhig gelegenem Stellplatz. Mit Pool und sauberen Sanitär Anlagen.
clean and tidy, good appliances, very comfortable stay. Value for money..and the pool was a bonus after a long drive. Thanks
Great place to stay
Very happy this park is pet friendly and has a large swimming pool. Beautiful clean accommodation and surrounds. Staff very friendly and helpful.|Highly recommended. Will stay again.
Dieser Campingplatz hat uns sehr gut gefallen. Freundlicher Service, die Anlage ist sauber und gut gepflegt. Und der Campingplatz ist gut gelegen, falls man den Mietwagen am Flughafen abgeben muss.

I had a great stay at Banksia Tourist Park! The location is convenient, making it easy to access shopping center (and the bus stop just in the front of the camping). The entire park is well-maintained. The caravan sites are spacious. The common areas are also well-kept and pleasant to use. The staff is friendly and always ready to help, which added to the overall positive experience.
The only downside was the mobile network, which wasn’t very reliable in certain parts of the park. Otherwise, everything else was nice, and I would definitely recommend it!

Best park I've been to in a long time.

The staff are very helpful and friendly.

Highly recommend. 🙌🙌🙌
Really good atmosphere!
A fantastic park in an excellent location, with exceptionally friendly and helpful staff. The gardens are beautiful, and our cabin was clean, well-equipped, and well maintained. Highly recommended.

We stayed here twice on our WA family holiday and was happy with our stay- we got a powered concrete pad- amenities are good shower pressure perfect nice and hot. Pool is nice, place is tranquil and maintained good- only slight downfall the washing machines are $7 a load I just think that's abit exy in price everywhere we stayed was $5.

My wife and I stayed in  the cabins here for 3 nights and I would not recommend it to anyone, we asked for a cabin with a queen bed and what we got was two single beds and mattresses pushed together, they were the most uncomfortable bed I've ever had the misfortune to sleep on,the air conditioner was very noisy and smelly,there was mould on some of the tiles in the bathroom and there was only one footstool for 2 people, we asked for a change of sheets as I accidentally scratched myself and smeared a small amount of blood on the bottom sheet,they said that they would leave the clean sheets in the cabin for us but when we got back they were nowhere to be seen, when I complained about it they couldn't care less,

Banksia are a mob of crooks and deserve zero stars.  On the afternoon of Monday November 10, 2025, we found ourselves in a new state having driven from Sydney and unable to get in contact with our original accommodation provider.  So we booked online and payed $585 for three nights in a cabin from Thursday 13, November, 2025.  The very next day, Tuesday November 11, we were contacted by our original accomodation, but when we phoned Banksia to cancel, we were told that our $585 wouldn’t be returned to us as no refunds are given seven days before the commencement of a booking.
Now, while I understand that if we had made the booking months in advance, and prevented other people from booking, their policy would be reasonable.  However, we had made the reservation the day before for two days hence, not inconveniencing anyone.
To be treated in such a way on Remembrance Day; a day still very important to many Australians for a number of pivotal events in our history.  It saddens me that this is such an obvious instance of unfair treatment by a business who did nothing, provided no service and took from working people $585.
I also note the owner of the Banksia, an ex investment banker, owns 90 such establishments and has no doubt lost touch with people who work and save for a holiday where every dollar matters.

The bed was 2 single beds pushed together, so the middle was really hard, the bed felt like its getting old. Lots of pillows, but they were all the same Hugh ones which didn't suit me either.
No toilet or shower in the room, but a useless lounge was there with a lot of wasted room which could've had a bathroom area.
They gave a WiFi password, but that didn't work either. Telstra & Vodafone reception is really bad too. Wouldn't recommend going there again.

You roll into Banksia after office hours and immediately feel like you have entered a campground that proudly refuses to join the modern world. To check in you need to call someone. Fine, except your phone has no Australian number because it is 2025 and the world has moved on. So you borrow a phone from a friendly local. The guy hands it over, you dial, and when he hears you have to call Brisbane just to open a gate in Perth he looks at you like you have told him you’re phoning the moon.

He is shocked.
You are embarrassed.
The whole situation feels like a comedy sketch written by someone who hates technology.

You call. You wait. You get put on hold. Someone transfers you. You wait again. At this point your borrowed phone is probably reconsidering its entire life.

Eventually staff arrives. They open a metal box like they are unlocking a cursed artifact and hand you an entry card plus a delightful pile of paperwork. Actual paperwork. On actual paper. The twenty first century is out there having a good time while this place is still treating check in like a tax audit.

Then comes the wisdom of ages
You should get an Australian SIM card.
Mate, people have been contacting each other worldwide for nearly two decades without caring about local numbers. WhatsApp exists. Email exists. Messenger exists. Your grandma’s fridge probably has WiFi.
But here at Banksia, if you do not have an Aussie number, you basically do not exist. Twenty years of travel and nobody ever cared. Until now. Congratulations Banksia, you win the Most Outdated Communication Policy award.

Now the WiFi.
Oh boy.
Technically they have it. But it only works if you stand near reception, hold your phone at a forty three degree angle and whisper a prayer to the router gods. On your site there is nothing. Not a flicker. So to check messages you take the pilgrimage to the lounge like some kind of wandering monk seeking digital enlightenment.

And then you go to the bathroom.
Locked.
Not with a keycard. Not with anything modern.
A chunky mechanical digi lock that looks like gear stolen from a Cold War safe house.
They cannot beam WiFi twenty meters but they secure the toilet like it contains crown jewels.

So you end up with this
Check in that requires calling another city.
WiFi that only exists in one sacred corner.
Paperwork like it is the Middle Ages.
A bathroom door that thinks it is guarding nuclear codes.

The campsite itself is fine. Nature is great. But Banksia is a beautifully chaotic paradox. Old tech. No tech. Spy tech. A place where you borrow a stranger’s phone to call Brisbane, but need a mechanical code just to take a leak.
Great place to stay for a couple of nights. We honestly mostly used to cook dinner and sleep so not sure about other facilities. It was comfortable for two people and very clean
Ein ruhiger, sauberer Caravanpark.
Great dog friendly cabins, great family get away that feels like you're far from the rat race with it being close

I think the location is quite close to the motorway exit and easy accessible.However, I was surprised that there is no shampoo, shower gel ..such amenities to provide.Also, there is way too many flys and mosquitos

Late check in took nearly one hour because we could not upload the photo ID because the reception is so bad. We kept trying and the link wo'nt work. They should change the system. We are so tired from 7hours drive then when we finaly got the room we just found out it's actualy share bathroom. We thought it was include with bathroom! I really need to have shower oh my god i have to walk far to the bathroom. I will never come back here ever again.

We had a nice grass site with power for our campervan. The shower was perfect, nice pressure and warm water. We didnt see or hear any dogs from the reviews. Arriving after hours; the woman on the phone is not friendly and very strict, but then the man from the park came to us and he was super super friendly.
Had everything we needed including an outside PowerPoint near the car port for charging electric vehicle. Easy key pickup & drop off out of hours

Absurd mirrors in female toilets, only for people 6ft tall. Nice to have a bath for my aching back, no grab handle near the bath, making it a very dangerous,  slippery experience.  Not one hook to hang towel or clothing near bath. Tiny parking space for caravan,  can hardly back in safely, signs regarding 'diapers' in toilets. We are in Australia not America,  thank God. Couldn't book online, they blocked us because we made a mistake in the measurements of our caravan so that we couldn't book. Have to show a photo ID, WiFi doesn't work, welcome to an unfriendly, sour, weird place.  Suppose a smile is out of the question. Never again.

Booked a powered site for 5 days only stayed 3. As much as I don’t mind dogs, way too many around, barking at all times they should have a section for dogs & caravans and another section for those that want to just relax. I didn’t get to sleep either, based on a main road could hear cars & trucks constantly going by. Had to leave earlier so went to reception (one of a few girls there) this one was rude when I asked if I could get a refund for leaving earlier her response “no” ok thanks, definitely won’t be going back there again waste of money better & nicer places to go. Won’t be recommending them.

My mum used our caravan for a weeks stay, was a horrible experience for her super noisey too close together, she needed to come back earlier to attend to my father who has Parkinson’s disease and they wouldn’t give her a refund  the 2 days she left earlier. Mind you they wanted her out at 10am when she had payed for that night.
She asked if she could stay in a cabin as we had to take the caravan and they asked her to pay full price instead of taken the price off the days she was gonna leave earlier.
Also on arrival we asked if we could park the car in a non used bay just for a hr or 2 and they wanted a nights stay amount just for parking there.
Also adding the internet was terrible couldn’t connect to anything nothing to watch or even go on the iPad. This place is a joke do not stay here.

Very poor service and won’t offer refund, I arrived after a red eye 7 hour overnight flight from Thailand at 9 am. Understood the check in listed was was not until 3pm  (even though the check out time is 10am?) but asked if I could check in early which they said I couldn’t and had to wait until 3pm. Waited 2 1/2 hours in the lobby and then saw all housekeeping the staff sat outside eating at 11:30 instead of getting a room cleaned and ready for me. Absolute joke. Have left and gone to Quest midland who gave me an early check in no problem as soon as I arrived. Avoid this place at all costs.

Let’s be clear — this is not a 4-star hotel, more like a 1 or 2-star motel. Don't know exactly how this place is rated 4 star.  No way in the world near that standard.

If you arrive after hours, good luck finding your cabin — no clear signs, no lighting, and very poor directions. The rooms are basic, with no toiletries like shampoo or shower gel. There’s even a note telling guests to wash their own dishes and messy room otherwise there will be additinal charges which is ridiculous for a hotel.

No daily cleaning, broken TV remote, and paper-thin walls where you hear everything from next door even a drop of a pin

On top of that, the reception keeps sending text messages throughout your stay, which just adds to the frustration. Instead of feeling welcomed, it felt intrusive and uncomfortable — like being constantly monitored rather than cared for.

For $150–$200 a night, it’s overpriced, noisy, and disappointing. Definitely not worth it — book somewhere else.

Very poor service, I arrived after a red eye 7 hour overnight flight from Thailand at 9 am. Understood the check in listed was was not until 3pm  (even though the check out time is 10am?) but asked if I could check in early which they said I couldn’t and had to wait until 3pm. Waited 2 1/2 hours in the lobby and then saw all housekeeping the staff sat outside eating at 11:30 instead of getting a room cleaned and ready for me. Absolute joke. Have left and gone to Quest midland who gave me an early check in no problem as soon as I arrived. Avoid this place at all costs
Clean, friendly and unlike some gday parks this one is well presented and well managed.
A great clean park. Camp kitchen a good size. Great hot water for the showers.
Nice area. Clean amenities!! One of the better parks we’ve stayed at.
A holiday of a lifetime

I felt like I was being scammed when dealing with this business. I was looking for a caravan park in a particular part of Perth. When I googled caravan parks for the suburb I wanted, this business came at the top of the list. After booking a site for a couple of nights, I realized that it was an hour away from where I wanted. Within 2 minutes of making the booking, I rang to cancel it. They refused. They seem to appear at the top of the list no matter where you search in Perth, so they rely on idiots like me not checking where they actually are and then refuse to give their money back. So after being stuck with staying there, I turned up at their reception to book in. They said that they wanted to see my driver's license to make sure I was who I said I was. So I showed them. However, they insisted on taking a copy of my license and all the details on it. We were in the process of travelling around Australia and had never been asked for this. The damage someone could do with my license and credit details was too great. So I refused their request to copy my license and they refused to let me stay at the park. So if you find this type of stuff strange, do go there.

*********** The park's reply below clearly shows that they are not the most trustworthy people to deal with. I understand cancellation policies and have no problem with them. That is they may have turned down other bookings and may find it difficult to get another customer. However, it is a stupid argument when it was only 2 minutes between the booking and the request to cancel it.

In regard to the license, they state in there reply that they only use it for verification purposes and do not store it or use it for other purposes. I had not objection to showing them my license and handed it to them. It was them taking a copy of it and storing that copy that I had an issue with. As they had already verified who I was by seeing the license, what purpose did the have for needing to store the information.

I never became aggressive to any of their staff as they claimed. I am a 67 year retired accountant. But we had an impasse. They would not let me stay without them keeping a copy of my license and I was not prepared to allow them to do so. It was this impasse that resulted in me not staying there and getting a refund. They make out that they refused to let me stay because I was aggressive. In fact they were more than willing to let me stay if I allowed them to take a copy of my license and use that copy for purposes unknown. There was nothing on their website or booking requirements stating I had to do this to stay there. So they refunded my money and I left.

We recently stayed at Banksia Tourist Park. The accommodation was average overall. The room was okay, clean enough for a short stay, but lacked basic amenities—there were no shower accessories provided, which was a bit inconvenient. On the plus side, car parking was secure at night, which I appreciated
A quiet, lovely park with nice staff, spacious sites and beautiful gardens.
Clean tidy and very friendly

We're regular guests, always coming with our dogs, staying in the cabin. Good big lawns/exercise areas for dogs. Never had any issues beside small ones, which were quickly fixed (eg. exhaust fan in the bathroom was blowing the air in instead of out). Each cabin has a spacious car port under a roof, which makes unpacking/packing the car easy on rainy days.

Andrea (and the other girl, so sorry I didn't get your name!!) were super helpful and welcoming despite our last minute booking. Amazing staff and all around a great warm friendly place to stay :) . Tysm - Ki
Close to midland easy to anywhere else . Bushy and had sitings of a Queda , a funny looking bilby thing.

Not impressed… Permanent residents sites all over the park some fairly untidy, tattered tarps covering annexes and campers, multiple cars on a few sites partially blocking lanes, loud cars leaving at 5-6am every day inc weekends, one lovely couple had to move sites because wind chimes had kept them up all night, another lovely couple just up and left because they were placed in front of the dump point when there was a multitude of other spots available, many people struggled to reverse into their sites on arrival when park was slightly filling up, having to ask other people to move their cars, we had too so we could hitch our van on due to site placement and having no room to manoeuvre without asking. They mention free WIFI available which yes it’s free but it’s also a capped data rate of 3000mb in case anyone was wondering/needs it… Thank god for starlink. You couldn’t pay us to stay there again. Not worth half as much as the rates they charge.
Staying for awhile in our caravan it's one of the best we've stayed at in Perth,  a quiet ,clean well run park

Reception staff were kind and helpful, but I was turned away despite vacant powered and unpowered sites — simply because I sleep in my car.

It feels discriminatory to exclude travellers based on vehicle setup. Not everyone has a rooftop tent or campervan, and they shouldn’t need one just to stay the night.

You would think that there would be some flexibility with their refund policy when you are able to produce a death certificate for an immediate family member who passed a day before your stay , but no. Disgusting customer service.

Pet friendly cabins were so run down and gross filthy park smelt like urine over 1500 for a week stay couldn’t sit on lounge was mortified had to buy my own antibacterial mop I called up as we have 2 frenchies we couldn’t stay in the newer chalets not even paying a bigger bond that I offered I asked were they clean and nice reception said yes so I booked the week it was gross they need to be knocked down and rebuilt Telstra internet almost none Optus worse
I put puppy pads down and walked on them as floor  was so filthy didn’t get any more photos we were out all day to avoid it showered at night and went to bed

As a guest in a caravan With our own facilities i am deeply disappointed by multiple things this park has to offer. For how expensive this park is. There is no heaters. Or at least fans in the facility and not to mention the rude individual (certain front desk Worker) si came across when i first checked in for my stay. I would NOT recommend coming to this family park if you have kids and expect unlimited wifi as is offered from others parks for any long of stays. For how expensive this place was for only a week I wouldn’t of expected they skimp out and only offer 700mb of data that lasted a night with the kids. The only decent thing about this park is something they dont control. The wildlife . Parrots and little QUENDA. But overall would NEVER pay another dime to that there ever again. Highly disappointed..

Great place to stay.
The toilets could be cleaned more regularly.
Otherwise very comfortable and friendly staff.

I felt that they had an something going on that was a scam. I was looking for a caravan park in a particular area in Perth. I don't know Perth well at all, so I relied on Google to find the correct park. This park came at the top of my list for the location I was looking for. Immediately after booking I sought directions to it. It was an hour from the location I was after. Within 2 minutes of making the booking, I rang them to cancel the booking. They refused. Now stuck with taking there, I went to reception to book in. They stated that they wanted to see my driver's license to make I was who I said I was. So I showed the license. But then wanted to copy it and I thought this was going to far. A lot can be done with a person's license details as well as their credit card details. Why did they want my license number and details for. It had nothing to do with having a caravan site for 2 nights. I refused to allow them to take a copy and they refused to let me stay at the park.|There is something very suspicious about the operators of this park.
We trialled a campervan and stayed at Banksia. Lovely location lots of greenery and trees. Great hot flowing showers.
The 3pm check-in was ridiculous especially when they still had the usual 10 am checkout. The staff were very regimented.

Rooms are small mining camp style for >$100 per night. No breakfast or restaurant available.

Service at checkin was pathetic
Arrived 5.35pm and could see the receptionist but door locked as they close at 5.30pm. Took over 20mins to use the call system for after hours checkin as there was another customer who arrived a minute before me.

Staff annoy you with SMS messages.

Location average.

Avoid.

We checked in after 6pm, reception was closed and the person on call was not able to check us in because we had a foreign phone number. It took us a while to stand outside but a gentleman arrived and handed over the keys.|Great stay. Have everything that you need. Small room but comfortable. |Few ants were seen in the room.|Good for only spending a night or few hours.|Kitchen is equipped.

Pet friendly! Nice clean cabin. The park surrounds are well kept too. The amenities also nice. Except the laundry block. That Maytag dryer looks like a relic of the 1980's. The general area beyond the park, not so much. Cabin bathroom exhaust fan needs some more horsepower. Toilet paper scarce. No need for an alarm clock. The first flight out of Perth goes overhead at 5am. All up, a nice place. Do read the cancellation policy on the website though. It's fair but challenging in terms of its credit policy. You can't give an accommodation credit to someone else. Just in case perhaps you didn't want to visit Perth again within 14 months of your last visit. Just sayin'.
Very nice park.
Website says they have unpowered sites and tent sites. Spoke to them they don't accept tents and dont do undowered sites opposite to the website.
Beautiful RV park, very well maintained with large sites and easy access.

Stayed in a Cabin with ensuite, did not camp. Clean, modern and with aircon. Felt secure as only people with a pass open the boom gate for vehicle entry. Well maintained site. Quite location in May, although during school holidays it could be different. The check in time of 3 pm, ensured I had to sit around for 5 hours. The usual check in time of 2 pm would have saved my one less hour of pain. Close to Perth, yet it still has a country vibe to it. Close to Midland shopping area.
Could not fault this place, excellent customer service, our beautiful four legged baby girl just love the walks and chilling outside , definitely coming back, thank you for everything .
Nice campgroud with good and clean facilities. Staff was very friendly and helpful. Bus stop close to the entrance.
The shop needs to reopen and sell more than soap powder and ice creams

The park itself is nice . Toilets are clean . Nice grass site , but don’t get a site near the ablutions as the smell is terrible . Hard to cook your dinner with that around.
People and dogs are not very considerate and let it poo on our site . The owner of the dog or the caravan park were not keen to clean it up .

In response to your feedback I wish I had taken a photo of the poo to prove I am telling the truth . Mind you a few visitors managed to step in it and the one  permanent advised us it was there before we trod in it .
I did advise the after hours number as it was during the holiday break but no reply from two messages .
As for the smell , we were the closest to the septic covers behind the bins C1
I’m sorry to say it seems your social media skills are more about deflecting rather than consideration

As a regular visitor, I stop by every month after work, for a night or two. Despite booking a grass site well in advance, I was given a sandpit, which appears to serve as a dog toilet, as indicated by the dog feces present. My booking specified a site with power and water, but the water tap was missing. When I inquired about its location, the front desk staff member responded with extreme rudeness, telling me to use the ablution water if needed and to complain if dissatisfied. Following their advice, I am complaining about their unprofessional behavior and poor customer service. Given the park's rates, I find the experience disappointing. Regrettably, due to the public holiday, I had no alternative but to stay. I will not be returning.

As a regular visitor, I stop by every month for a night or two, after I finish my swings at work. Despite booking a grass site well in advance, I was given a sandpit which is served as a dog toilet, as indicated by the dog feces present. My booking specified a site with power and water , but the water tab was missing. When I enquire about its location, the front desk staff responded with extreme rudeness, telling me to use the ablution water if needed and to complaint if dissatisfied. Following her advice I am complaining about her unprofessional behaviour and poor customer service. Given the parks rate I find the experience disappointing. Regrettably due to the public holiday, I have no alternative but to stay. I will not be returning.
Look elsewhere
Clean and tidy park. Some spots are a challenge to back the van in. Both Telstra and Optus have very poor reception here.

Lovely quiet very clean and tidy park
Large concrete sites.  Plenty of room

Enjoyed our stay very much

Booked for 2 nights, travelling from Qld, needed to cancel booking sent email 24 hrs prior. Received call I requested 1 night refund was refused. Called back informing I have a friend travelling through who could use my booking, no, as booking cancelled and no spots available. Worst attitude from a park in all my years of caravanning. Hope they choke on my $66 per night overpriced charge.
Stayed in a cabin for the weekend, they are clean and have all the amenities you need (ovens are in camp kitchen) and is pet friendly.

We’ve stayed with our car/campervan for over a night and we booked it last minute as we were on a rush and everything was fine. They’ve booked us a site in front of the WC which was handy, all the amenities where very clean. I’d recomend it!
Caravan sites are tight and poorly designed but they are very clean/tidy and quiet. The amenities are good and clean. TV reception is very good. Telstra internet is almost non-existent.
Great place to stay, friendly as staff, has everything you need bbq area, pool, park for kids and is central to everything, we will be back

I booked a powered site I asked for early check I'm and close to ammenities staff were helpful and contacted me when my site was available. Nettled in beautiful surroundings with a lovely pool area.. bbq and shared kitchen games room ammenities and grounds are kept clean and tidy. 10 min from shopping centre nice quiet secure park. If your a g’day member take advantage of the great discounted rate.. pet friendly the wildlife arent shy at coming for a visit either..

Brilliant park, very well kept and the pool is awesome! The annuals who live here are kind and great customer service up at reception who accommodated our extra night due to us forgetting we had to leave. Thank you!

A really nice camping ground with good sites and all the facilities. Well located. We drove to midland station and caught the train everywhere. Camp kitchen and lounge are great. Lovely swimming pool. Beautiful well maintained gardens. Friendly staff.

Amazing stay! We Loved the spacious room, it had a lovely little set up. Fridge in the room was great as I had 2 under 2 that required lots of food & snacks on hand.The pool was a big bonus & the kids park was also enjoyed by my boys. Looking forward to revisiting

We booked a caravan site for the weekend.  Very well maintained park well looked after. All amenities were lovely and clean. The girls at the reception were very nice and greeted me with a very nice smile. Thankyou for allowing us to check in early. Muchly appreciated. Would definitely stay again.

Great place to stay, please go easy on the little dog policy next time, A little goes a long way for the happiness of a pet owner. All in all a great place to stay close to everything that you need as a traveler. I will return and do recommend you visit this place for a happy stay around safe travels 👍🤪🐶🇦🇺🇦🇺🇦🇺

Booked a camper site with water for 2 nights, site had no water, luckily we still had enough onboard to use.  Campsite we were allocated backed directly onto a sewage settling pond. Just what you want to smell.🤢
Booked 2 nights, stayed one. Won’t be back.
Surely there must be some health regulations regarding being able to let people camp so close to a sewage pond.

Edit to response.
Please see photo showing water included in site booked.
Nice clean park in a good location. Pity about the grey water pond smell. We sat inside on our last night.
Great facilities for families. Nice clean rooms, modern & comfortable. Keep your eyes out for the bandicoots!

I had a lovely experience at Banksia the other day. I arrived late at night and had to call the after hours number , the gentleman was so accommodating and helped me out. The unit and amenities were spotless and very comfortable. I highly recommend Banksia Tourist Park to anyone looking for a friendly welcoming place to rest 🌺

2 nights in Feb 2025, a group of 3 adults.

Pros: close proximity to the airport & Swan valley! Common kitchen, playground, barbecue areas & swimming pools were all available. Individual cabins, privacy ensured. Quiet area, so we had pretty good sleep at night. Not to forget, staffs were friendly & helpful!

Cons: Cabin can be a little squeezy for a group of 3, would be fine for couple & solo traveller. The kitchen is small just right for simple cooking (no cooking oil could be found even in the common kitchen, so we bought one & left it there for public sharing). Also, bring your own toiletries as only soaps were provided.
We love staying in our caravan at Banksia Tourist Park 👍😀 it is a great location. Very clean amenities and lovely gardens with lots of trees 🌳 The staff are lovely... we will be back

Extremely rude staff. You pay 50$ to join Gday parks and then they ban you for life and cannot provide an explanation. I’d say racism would be my guess. Do not stay here. You can book drive all the way there and be turned down on check in leaving you stranded. Avoid this park at all costs. You may be turned down on check in without explanation and sleeping in the street
Great campsite to get away from the busy town!

This was the best site that we stayed in around Perth. We had a powered campervan site, sadly just for one night. The site is clean and well maintained. Lots of flower beds and lawn,  and hardstanding was in good order. The swimming pool was a real bonus. Barbecues were clean and plenty of seating under shade. The toilet block was spotless and modern, water was hot.
Always polite and friendly staff. The accommodation is well presented and cared for it is also close to places we need to go to.

Great clean caravan park. Toilets are in good condition and clean. Plenty of on lead exersice areas for dogs. There is also an off lead enclosed dog park about 4 mins away in bushmead (greenslope dog park).
Stayed for a week and found my experience to be very satisfying and enjoyable. These little Quenda were so friendly and cute.

We always go here it’s a quiet spot
Love the quiet location

Lovely stay here. This park turned out to be a little gem. Nice sites. Nice public areas, clean neat and tidy.
We travelled overseas for 3 weeks and came back here to unwind while we recuperated.  Thank you

My partner and I travelled with our doggo from the Pilbara and stayed 1 night.
The park is beautifully maintained and well laid out with good, clean amenities. We staid in a pet friendly cabin that was clean, equipped with everything you need and the bed was comfy. Doggo also had a great night sleep.
I had messaged the park to inquire as to extending our stay for a night to which I was advised they usually only accept small/medium dogs (ours is a large breed) which surprised me as I couldn’t understand the reasoning behind this with past experience from smaller dogs being much more aggressive and also the assumption of our dogs behaviour. This is also not stipulated on booking.com in detail. That said I was very thankful they agreed to honour our existing booking. The after hours check in process is tedious with 3 calls made to get the safety deposit box code for our keys. You call, while
St on the call they send you a link to upload your ID - this could fast track this process by sending the link with booking confirmation? We also noticed 4 other tenants going through the same process so this process does not stop everyone just grabbing there key when someone eventually gets through and gets the safe open… Room for improvement with this process.
When we did eventually get our key, we promptly locked ourself out of our cabin due to  a combination of fatigue after a 12 hr drive and a dodgy cabin door lock. The care taker was extremely prompt in coming round to open it up for us!
We’d love to stay again but unfortunately we love travelling with our large breed dog as he is very much part of the family.

Absolutely fantastic. Clean. Quite. Comfortable. Staff were very helpful and ground team very friendly.
Thankyou   We will be back
Nice quiet clean caravan park and the pool was a bonus

Disgusting 😡 i was turned away because my 7 year old gentle giant of a dog was crossed with a german shepherd this park needs to put in practice's of meeting the pet's before refusing entry just pitiful P.S I was rejected because of the breed of my dog not because of its size, i was told because he is classed a viscous breed not because he is they cant have him in there, a shitzu is more viscous than my dog zero stars for professionalism

Campsites are a good size and the pool is clean.  However, there isn’t much that you can do.  The advertised games room costs and the pool table can’t be used after 5:30pm.  Camp kitchen gets locked at 9pm and opens at 7am (no early breakfast).  Free wifi is 1gb per campsite and then you have to pay.  Pool closes at 7pm and the playground can’t be used after sunset.  Paid $175 for one night for two camp sites so was super disappointed with what’s on offer and the time constraints given the cost.  Wouldn’t recommend or stay again.
Loved the place, it was just me and my dog. Quiet, friendly, lovely cabin, good location. Will definitely be back.

Stay here a lot, from the cabins to the units.
Always clean and good facilities.
Park for kids is nice, and swimming pool is good for a dip.
Very nice new bbq area near the pool and nice grassed area.
Staff always helpful and friendly.
Highly recommended.
Booked in as a family of 11 and had the best time swimming in the pool, using the camp kitchen and BBQ. Great activity park for the young ones to play in too.
Had a great stay, rooms are smaller than they appear in photos but super cute, cosy & clean. Will definitely stay again. Great value for price & friendly staff.
I only had a short stay, but I enjoyed it. The chalet was very clean. I would stay there again.

We booked this stay on booking.com and stayed in a cabin with our dog.
The cabin was lovely and clean and just big enough for two med-large adults and a pooch.
The grounds were very tidy and clean. The neighbours seemed lovely (permanent residents) and there were lots of space out the back to walk my pooch. There was even a pool although I didn’t use it!
The park is nice and close off the highway so was a good location without feeling you are right in the hustle and bustle.
We stayed in the second story apartment and it was beautiful, however there's limited internet and checking in after hours is a pain.

Pre booked (5 months in advance) and paid in full for a site to fit a 26' van, 8.2mtr. Arrived after hours 5:30pm drive to our site, 7m x 7m pad. Cannot open the awning, draw bar hanging on to the road. Called and got a phone operator who suggested we contact the office at 10am in the morning. Disappointed would be an understatement.

Contrary to the response received from banksia group, some conflict may have resulted from a difference of what was booked and what the banksia tourist park received. As stated above, we pre booked a large site. The after hours number was called at 5:30pm as stated and the response was contact us on the morning. This was not resolved in a timely manner, banksia did not do all they could to rectify the situation. The on site staff were wonderful, 'the next day' and they upgraded our site to an ensuite, much appreciated. But DO NOT put this on us, if banksia have an issue with their booking system that needs to be rectified rather than the blame game for a poorly operated tourist park.
The Banksia team accommodated to my needs with after hours check in, communication was great, the cabin was well presented. Thank you Banksia park.
Highly recommend. The chalet was clean and beautifully presented with everything we needed. Will definitely be staying next time we are in Western Australia
Perfect place for pets. Locals are all very friendly.

If I could give zero stars I would. Never again. We booked and arrived late afternoon. When we got to out accommodation it was not clean and the shower curtain hasn't been changed in a long time which made the whole room smell like sour milk. We rang the after hours number and the care taker came out to see us. The room had a really bad smell and the caretaker went and got us a new shower curtain and a can of air freshener. He told us he cant do much more and was sorry. We couldn't stay in this room so we had to find other accommodation down the road. We contacted the manager there and asked for our refund and he refused to give us a refund. The manager told us that there was nothing wrong with the room and he went to the laundry room and sniffed all the shower curtains to find our one that smelled like sour milk but none of them had any odour to them and he inspected the room and found nothing wrong. I asked for my complaint to be taken further but the managed told me he is it and he makes all the decisions and wont be changing his mind. it was a lot of money they took off for the time we had booked there.

This is one of the better tourist parks I have stayed at. Clean, nice gardens and trees can make for a relaxing stay. Only gave it 4 stars due to location, only one ablution block can be difficult when the cleaners are there and you need to go.... Also the proximity of the camp kitchen is not centrally located.

We did a late check in and they didn't give us the code to get in and we spent 25 minutes waiting on the phone at the entrance barrier to be answered asking me for personal information that they could have asked me at the time of booking, not at 10 pm when after driving for 10 hours all we wanted was to get there and get in. They kept us at the gate without letting us in for all that time. we have been travelling in caravans for more than 2 months and on many occasions late check out had never happened. There is very little lighting in the park. The toilets were dirty and the showers were dirty too. Not recommended.

We Stayed at Banksia Tourist Park for 4 night booking and Checking in was so easy the reception were amazing, Caravan sites spacious,A shout out to the cleaners of the Ablution blocks cleanest we have seen in our travels highly recommended this Park
Great park...Telstra service in the park area isn't t the best unfortunately

Excellent Caravan Park! We were here for 2 weeks and had a large site, with a great area to park our large car also a nice double slab.Beautiful grassed areas, for walking your dogs.
The amenity blocks were spotlessly clean, as were the camp kitchen and BBQ area. The laundry was nice and clean with plenty of line space to hang clothes.
The kids play and lawn area was huge and the pool looked very inviting, just a bit cool though, but amazing in summer I'm sure.
Office staff were very polite and helpful.
Only one criticism would be the bins. Theses are only emptied once per week and they are usually overflowing after day two. You can see the wildlife going through them at night, which I'm sure is not good for them.  A few more rubbish bin empties/ or more bins would be perfect. Also maybe some smaller bins for the cans 10c refund. These could then be donated to help any charity really. Telethon, soup kitchen, Men's shed, Playgroup etc

We would not hesitate to book here again and already we have recommended to other Grey Nomads travellers and will continue to do so!

Great park, keep up the good work team!!
We stopped here for 8 night and had a lovely stay. Very friendly campsite with lovley facilities.
Always great price and close to everything I have had to go

Banksia Park is very well maintained. The camping areas are nice, and the caravan sites are well paved amongst lovely gardens. This park is conveniently located central to the Perth region, so if you are wanting to explore around Perth this park is a great option. Highly recommended park for a quiet and comfortable stay.
Great place to stay very convenient to get around Perth, facility are good and clean.

Just spent 7 nights at the Banksia Tourist Park with our caravan. The park itself is extremely clean, very pretty with amazing gardens and plants and an abundance of wildlife. The parking plot was in an excellent position with extremely easy access and quiet. Very little passing traffic and friendly, welcoming neighbours. The facilities are wonderful, great pool, fully equipped Camp Kitchen with everything you could think of to cook any type of meal. Lovely clean hot showers and the staff are friendly and extremely efficient. Will definitely be back....Well worth a visit.....

We are still there. The constant drip of leaking pipes in the wall of unit H meant ww got little sleep for the two nights so far. Thank god we are only there another night before we leave this horrible place. |The long term residents are downright scary.

We stayed on a powered site, and the park was nice & peaceful.  Amenities were conveniently located, and staff were helpful.
Thanks
Davedani Downunder

Thanks for the opportunity to comment, we originally booked for 2 days & stayed 4, enjoyed the facilities very helpful staff, clean & tidy area, clean amenities block & a great camp kitchen, we will definitely return next trip around Australia.😊😊😊😊
Nice quiet park close to a golf course. Amenities are clean and the washing equipment is in good order. Lots of permanents but secure and quiet. 20 minutes to CBD. Trains nearby.

Great location! Quiet and peaceful safe place to stay
And really reasonable price!
Staff are truely amazing very thoughtful and welcoming… last few stays we haven’t made the night due to delays from flights and they always check in the following day to ask if we were okay!
Thanks Peeps at Banksia tourist park
One of our favourites to come stay

We stayed 2 nights, had no hot water, for our whole stay, tried reporting to after hours with no results. checkin was a very long process after hours (could be an easier process if license link ect was sent out before you arrive). Location was good.

Old & out dated room.
Ask for early check in (3pm check in time) but never accommodated.
On top of daily rate, another non refundable $10 per dog to stay.
Also, we had txt msg asking for any early departures to txt back, staying keys had been dropped off in security box by txtn "BYE".
Not sure what that was about. Won't be back.

It was our first stay and we would highly recommend Banksia Tourist Park, we took our dachshund Frankie, it is pet friendly and a beautiful quiet park a lovely swimming pool and very close to Kalamunda definitely we will return.
The lady at reception went out of her way to help us out. Fantastic.
Beautiful gardens
Room was clean and comfortable, staff were friendly and helpful and text messages made checking in and out a breeze

I have stayed here multiple times a month since march 2024. The caravan sites are large and concrete, with good facilities. The cabins are luxurious and have everything you need.  Staff are always friendly and helpful, location is perfect. 10mins from airport, 10 minutes from midland or high Wycombe.  Perfect for fifo workers like myself. It’s that good, Ive booked ahead right up till January 2025

Highly recommend Banksia Tourist Park. One of the best maintained parks we have stayed at. Gardens are a credit. Staff exceptional. We had a van site, however cabins looked well kept & a majority are new & modern. Amenities spotless. Would definitely stay again & highly recommend.

Loved staying at this park. My favorite park in Perth so far.It was so quiet and private, the trees and plants were really beautiful and  the birds. Seeing the quender was a real highlight.  I didn't really want to leave. I highly recommend this caravan park 👌
Caravan site was a good size and park was very quiet and not far from Perth city centre,definitely stay there again
Beautiful, well maintained park. Also spotted mother and baby Quenda on our site.
Very freindly, lots of good sites, especially grassed camping ones. Ablutions a bit dated but clean otherwise would be 5 stars. Great camp kitchen and BBQ area.
Wonderful Caravan Park. Great amenities
Good place to stay.
Excellent service,  fantastic value for money for locals with, very convenient for the airport and the city. Clean and comfortable.
Lovely well kept park.
Absolutely fantastic pet friendly place. The cabins are great and we loved having all our fur babies with us. Awesome dog exercise area. Great playground for the kids.
We had a very pleasant stay. The amenities were very clea and the staff are friendly

We have stayed here on numerous occasions with our caravan and also stayed in cabins. We have 2 small dogs and they have a couple of cabins catering for dogs. They are beautifully kept, no dog smells  and just lovely. The powered caravan sites are roomy for your caravan
Very pricey for what it was!
Very well kept park. Our first site was a bit tight but had no trouble moving to a big and better one. Facilities are great and staff very professional.
Very nice, quiet location. Young girl at reception was very good.
Staff very friendly and helpful. Park is very tidy and the gardens are beautiful. The ablution block is very clean and well maintained. We will definitely stay again.
Great park for our base to explore Perth. Good amenities & decent space between caravan sites.

I had just arrived in Midland and looking for one night accommodation. From the time my call was answered to the time I left they were absolutely wonderful ensuring there would be no problems. I stayed in their donga which was clean and lush compared to the remote dongas I have stayed in. Thank you so much for supporting a very tired traveller.
Nice park  bit of airport noise, Shower block old but clean bit pricey school holidays the cost of the park.

Excellent park, well run, clean, friendly and helpful staff, (thank you to Emily and the reception team) good size sites, (plenty of room for a 8mtr long Winnebago and a car dolly side by side) plenty of room for big rig at waste dump, rubbish bins regularly emptied never saw an overflowed bin, the only park I've seen with bath tubs aswell as showers, good water pressure and temperature, problems delt with quickly and professionally, stayed around a week was able to extend stay no worries, highly recommended park. Cheers Mick

3 stars this time at best. Ensuite was not working and was dated. Reported problems to admin 2 times re toilet not flushing but they did nothing. Amongst other items also reported like an abandoned car battery on our site. Reported that 4 times and at first time it was reported told let us know again next day - like its my responsibility as a quest to remind your admin as they are too busy woupd seem to action. Had to use main abolutions for park which questions why we paid for ensuite if you can't fully use it. Ensuites also very dated with no exhaust fans to expell water vapour. When it rained there was no down pipe from gutter so rain pours directly on you at opening of ensuite door. No light either at night so had to place my own as much as our neighbours did the same.

Park was dog friendly but after we booked and paid told on arrival. Sorry, that will be another $2 per day. Not saying that we realise there are costs but a little bit stingy after you get there to be told pay extra when you already advised having a dog on their booking  site when paying for accommodation. Whilst conditions say there is a cost no amount is mentioned?

What got worse is on arrival you got 1 and 1/2 toilet rolls to last a week and a bit. Then told if you want anymore, it would be $2 a role!!! Funny how you pay more for ensuite site which as mentioned above was only half working, yet can go to the general park abolutions and not be charged for this. A bit of a rip off and seemed very penny pinching.

Park is full of permanents, and whilst you clearly state speed limits in park appears they can race around park like a speed demon without care!!!

Overall experience was worst of parks we have stayed in. If admin did their job instead of fobbing things off then maybe it would have been more enjoyable.

Park also very noisy given location to airport and busy road but will give that it is an airport but expect plane take off noise 24/7.

Lastly was surprised to see 2 shirtless persons walking around the park at night. 1 of them had the gall to ask us for park internet code and when asked if staying in park said yes. Unfortunately didn't give us reason to feel safe after this instance and worry if anything was going to get knocked off.

Won't be back ever for above reasons as don't think cost of stay is value for money.

Also in response to the parks comments. We had better things to do than discuss items already raised when we were there, some raised multiple times, therefore not sure what the purpose of the call was going to achieve. It was clear when you called you were only trying to save face. Just fix items for your next guests. Unless you were going to give a partial refund then there was no point talking. Also I was driving so my safety  was more important than your call. Think hanging up was justified as pointless you calling in first place to justify poor service and poor action on items raised when we were there, nor worth our valuable time again to discuss these items. My wife also told you when you called it was not a great time.

Also feel feedback was justified and factual so not changing it.
It's a really nice place, the bed is comfortable and the staff is friendly.

Booked 5 days out, received confirmation of booking, payment taken, only to be aggressively confronted upon arrival, for missing personal information, that was never mentioned in the text messages, or emails confirming my booking. My wife booked for me, as she usually does for my regular work trips, and this was a massive problem to the entitled person at the desk. I was very nearly homeless for the night, which probably wouldn’t have been too bad, after finally getting to my room, and being kept awake for most of the night by the noisy aircon unit that actually shook the room at times. I’ll sleep under a bridge before heading to that joint again. Never felt less welcome anywhere, and I stay in a lot of different hotels, and parks, beware people
We stayed 2 nights on a powered site and the Banksia Park is wonderful. The sites are fully concreted which is great. The park is very clean and well maintained. Thank you.

Fine place. We stayed in a cabin, which was clean and perfectly acceptable. Pullout couch was not made up for a bed despite having 4 people on the reservation, but that wasn't a huge deal as all the sheets and things were provided. Kind of annoying check in since we were after hours, we had to call a number, who sent us a link to put pics of drivers license for me, except the service was pretty bad and we had no WiFi since we hadn't checked in yet, so it took a bit to go through so we had to wait outside in the dark. Otherwise, staff were helpful and kind. Our door to the cabin didn't lock, which was a big safety concern. Door was cracked open on arrival, and then would "lock", but was easily wiggled open with minimal effort. We ended up sliding the table in front of the door for the night. Comfortable enough bed and bathroom, but in a bad location if you don't have a car. Not really near anything. Probably wouldn't stay again, but could recommend to those with camper or caravan needing a place to sleep for a few hours before hitting the road again.

Although arriving late can be very tedious due to security measures to hand over the key, it also generates a lot of peace of mind knowing that only the right people are staying in the accommodation. Beyond that, the non ensuite cabin that we had was sooo clean and have everything you need for a night or even more. We were able to cook and the amenities were also sparkling clean along with all the park facilities. We arrived on a rainy night and we have a roof for parking that helps a lot when you have to walk into the cabin. Huge congratulations to the cleaning and maintenance team for doing such a brilliant job. You feel that you are really paying for a worthwhile service. I would return and I am already recommending it.

We did a test run with our new campervan.  Site was level surrounded by beautiful trees and birds. Lots of beautiful native plants. Toilets and spacious showers were very clean and close to our site. The bath was big. People were very pleasant and helpful.
I stay here all the time awesome park

We booked ahead for site 1 because of the easier access but when we turned up we were told by the very rude receptionist that we were now on site 26 - I asked why & was told it was given to someone else & that management had the right to do that - by management she seemed to imply herself!  I asked about other sites that were easier to get into & was told none were available for me & I could either take site 26 or another right in amongst the permanents, or leave!||The park is 95% permanents with more being built (2 storey) - apart from sites 1-13 which are on the end, the other sites are on very narrow roads jammed in among the permanents. - reversing is a nightmare!  Most of the permanent residents are good but there are a couple of ferals with the loud music, tv & swearing.||The park is easy to get to via the freeways.  However, it is situated next to the Roe freeway & it is very noisy. - you can hear it anywhere in the park.  It also fronts onto another busy road.  We chose this park because it is close to the airport & it is - but that means the planes fly over the park at times quite low.  All in all a noisy park & you will need good earplugs esp if you get put next to the ferals!||We will not stay there again!
The staff here are all so friendly and helpful. The gardens are beautiful and the location for me, was ideal. Thank you for making my stay super comfortable
Booked 3 days stayed 4 days, powered site, nice park very tidy amenities clean service was good very friendly I would definitely stay again

The units were so beautifully done up inside we wanted to stay, gorgeous. Having two tvs was the best as hubby & I watch different shows at night. So nice & clean and all was lovely to touch. Will definitely be our number one stay when we come back to the hills to visit our family. The lovely staff in the office were great. Thank you so much, ten out of ten from Hubby & I 🏆💐
Great caravan park
Went to check it out as my next camp destination,looks great

Awesome place to stay, clean and tidy chalets and accomodating for travelling with a doggo, staff are always helpful and attentive, I enjoyed it so much I have stayed there multiple occasions for past 4years and will continue to do so
Cabins are very clean and grounds tidy.

Found management to be very accommodating and also provided us with late check out.
Facilities were clean and nicely presented.

We had a wonderful stay at Banksia Tourist Park. The service was great, Price great, facilities were really good, amenities, clean, and they even have a bath in amenities block. How cool is that. And we also got to see one or two bandicoots at night.
Nice and clean well set out reasonable priced.

The chalet was excellent, lots of room, well equipped and very clean.
Only downside was the unusable WIFI.

We took our van Flo to Freedom for a few nights as we needed to be in Perth for a family matter. The park, it's facilities, the people and the service were all first rate. It provided a welcome refuge at the end of each day. We loved it and highly recommend the park as a result.
Did not stay in the roomsbut the service is great and everything is clean 👌

Worst caravan park I've been to. Was accused of domestic violence after my partner accidentally dropped a pole on his foot while trying set up our camper. The following day my step daughters took our dog to the BBQ area and my partner called out to them, I was immediately called with more accusations of dv. I've never been involved in a domestic and neither has my partner. We were staying there with our 7 week old son and these ridiculous accusation have caused serious issues for our family.  They have keypad locks for the ablutions block but the women's are never locked from either side. They also have a massive "pond" not a pond. It's a grey water run off and it stinks. They also have little regard for their permanent residents, scattering their homes through out camp sites, forcing them to listen to campers come and go, and all the noise that comes with them.
Nice place, lovely staff, swimming pool big and clean.

We are grey nomads travelling Australia
Banksia tourist park was a treat for us,  fully concreted sites for car and van
Great bathroom and laundry facilities
Getting into sites can be tight but if you can tow a van you should be able to reverse well also
Lovely quiet  and clean park
Would go back again
Nice and quiet place , with the large on suite and powered site. Clean! Would recommend it

Not a "tourist" park.

This park is mainly permanent sites, cabins etc. The remaining sites are nearly all concrete or paved.

My experience was that I booked months ahead and requested a good site. What I got was the smallest site they had.  It was meant for a campervan or similar as there wasn't room for a 14-foot van, our awning, and our car. It took some convincing, but eventually, we got a bigger site. The staff did continue to complain that I should have been more accurate about the size of my van.  Unfortunately, this site was under a massive gum tree. Continually dropping seed pods, this was not a pleasant experience.  Also, solid concrete, so there no place for awning pegs.

During our Easter stay, we tried to get another site for our son and his campervan. We were told fully booked and no space available.  Not even one night. Well, there were numerous sites vacant the whole time. We also saw really large drive-through sites with tiny vans in them.

The staff were also passive-aggressive.  Made us feel like we were intruding by staying there rather than paying guests.

If you are going to stay here, book ahead, tell them you have a 22-foot van and can't reverse. You'll get a drive-through site near the toilets. The staff won't come outside and check, and I'm sure they have never walked through the park.

Stayed in chalet G for 1 night, very clean and comfortable. Very quite park, had a walk around and was pleased to see gardens between the caravan sites. Sitting outside in the evening and had a visit from a couple of the parks permanents. Little Furry Qaeda's.
Situated in a pleasant park, unfortunately if like me you don't have a car it's not very convenient.WiFi is completely non existent nice swimming pool though.

Stayed on a powered site, and it was really near to the amenity block (toilet and laundry) which was a great point for us. There were many perm residents which formed a little community looking out for each other.

Landed in a private estate and had quite a time to get out of the tight spot with our motorhome but the residents there helped us a bit. Maybe the reception should just mentioned to follow the signs instead of saying to turn at the 2nd turns haha.

The toilet/showering lighting can be improved - not sure the reason for yellow lights.

Felt safe in the area, highly recommended!

We booked a cabin here as we were meeting up with family
I booked a superior cabin and it was no better than a Donga on a mine site
Paid $400 for 2 nights
We we got there the place was filthy
Went to office and was told the housekeeper had gone home so would have to wait till the next day
Also asked about remote for tv Told it was on order and would have to use the buttons on the side of tv
Today we found a cockroach in the place this was after it was cleaned
Rude person in reception kept apologising but no offer of a reduction of costs
We really felt ripped off and will never return and spoilt our trip with family

Stayed here for 2 weeks in the chalet Motel style accommodation. Seemed like a lot of undesirable kind of people staying there, kind of felt like a hallway house or people after coming out of rehab or something. Didn't get much sleep. You can hear everything going on in the next rooms. Plus they are not self contained, if you plug in the electric hob it will most likely trip, then you have to wait about an hour for someone to come as trip board is locked. I use to pick up rubbish every morning outside and put it in the bin from the night before antics
Great place to stay.  Clean and comfortable, friendly staff. We often stay there.

For a family with young kids , we enjoyed our stay so much. The bbq area especially, kids loved the pool.  And loved the bunk beds.
We had a great time and we will be staying here every time we travel to perth.

This is one of our favourite tourist parks. The staff are friendly, the park is clean, great pool with partial shade, facilities are well kept. My partner works away and I work in our Winnebago, so I'm left on my own quite a bit, safety at a caravan park is huge to me. I felt extremely safe, not worried at all about theft or random people walking around. This is definitely a tourist park that we will keep coming back to, they set the bar high.

Drains blocked that smells
Ablation block clean
Staff quite rude and abrupt.

Have stayed in pet friendly cabins a number of times and have always had a good experience.
Cabins are clean and well laid out. The pool and grounds are well maintained and the staff have always been friendly and helpful

Power and water too far from some sites need in excess of 20m of cord, hose and drainage pipe. The drain near our site wouldn't drain and did overflow also had offensive odour. Maintenance staff really needs to check more often. Also had problem with Power cutting in and out again told it's a maintenance problem that they will look into, with temperature more than 40 degrees no air-conditioning is the last thing you want.
Amenities very clean and tidy.
Absolutely fantastic place

Overall, a good place to stay with lovely grounds, amenities and location. A few things to note:

Wifi was very limited and there was hardly any phone service in room so unable to stream shows etc, difficult with teenagers!
The chalet although clean still had lots of dust under the bed and on lamps etc. Prob needed a more thorough clean.
The sliding door was coming off the runner, could not open and close.

Thanks
Absolutely beautiful park will diffinitly be back highly recommend

We've stayed at several campsites over the last few weeks, and this is the best of them all. Fantastic showers,so powerful and always clean. Rec room and kitchens are great. Pool is very clean and great for both our 5 year old and 10 year old. Friendly and efficient staff. Thorough welcoming from the lady on reception. Great location for is heading to the airport early for a flight. Thank you Banksia ❤️

What a superb hidden gem this park is. Booked an ensuite site on a big concrete slab to accommodate our big van. Very clean and tidy everywhere. Quiet with lots of shade, bird life abounds. Good proximity to shopping, even Perth city is reasonably close, good access to freeways north and south. Emily at reception is a wonderful representative. Highly recommended.
Great swimming pool , park lovely and well maintained, staff great , highly recommend

Great amenities
Very quiet location
Love the park
Great caravan park! We have stayed here 4 times now and can not complain about a thing. Good clean amenities and very friendly staff!!

We were running late to check in but got a phone call from a lovely lady from the park to give me instructions about how to check in after hours. So polite and very helpful. Chalet was clean and so quiet! Would stay there again if we need accomodation in the city.

Very spacious room with separate lounge and a TV in both the lounge and the bedroom.
Great value, lively stay.

We arrived in our van late Friday afternoon with no issues checking in to a powered site. Super clean, tidy and well maintenance with washing facilities and amazing showering water pressure.

Have to add the pool is fantastic!!!

Well done management

Stayed for 3 nights in a powered site. Firstly the check in process was the most inefficient system I’ve experienced after going around the whole country. We got an email that said check in was after 3pm. We then got a text message the day of check in  at 2:45pm saying if checking in after 4pm to follow the instructions in text, but if checking in before 3pm check in at reception. When we got the reception it had shut at 3pm. The after hours check in process was appalling. There was a National number to call that took a long time on hold, when they didn’t answer I called the mobile number that was stated on the reception door for if there was no answer on the other number. The male on the mobile number said “I can’t help you, they’re busy, just keep calling and if you can’t get through leave a message”. I then continued calling the national number and got through and once they answered they required 100 points of ID over the phone before allowing me the safe number to get the swipe card. Secondly there was no water accessible to the site. Thirdly permanent residents using our site as a car park. The park had a very eery feeling with lots of permanent residents hanging around. Maybe the whole check in process just made it feel off but I wouldn’t be staying here again or recommending this park to anyone
The site's are clean and every thing else
Always a pleasure to stay at Banksia, stayed in cabin this time and it was very clean and bed was comfortable

The build quality and standard of facilities and level at which they are maintained is exceptional. Kitchen, lounges and BBQs very good indeed. Grass, pool and play area are also nice. Not much noise from traffic and can hardly hear planes from airport at all.

Clean and comfortable
Allows pets / allows pets to be booked in
Beautiful quiet park staff very helpful especially Emily in reception  rectifying an issue we had. Thank you. Had a beautiful stay👍👍👍
Home away from home
Clean room clean facilities
A great friendly place to stay
Visiting a friend ..

Super low rating from me due to not being able to make it to stay last night at this place, asked nicely if I could get a refund due to fact I had a car accident and was stuck a few hours away waiting to the next morning for a new rental car.  I was feeling really sad and stressed, wasn't rude at all.. had to pay $2K excess, another hotel, another hire car.... Spoke to 2 different ladies and didn't really feel much sympathy at all, they were just quoting refund policy and that I would have to email them for management to decide.  Surprise surprise email today quotes "They have advised that unfortunately a refund cannot be issued as per the cancellation policy.We thank you again for your understanding."

So disappointed that they couldn't make exception for my case,  won't book or recommend staying here.
Also upon reading other 1 star reviews I'm sure a  response will be posted from Banksia stating some excuse, probably quoting their policy,, can't wait....

Banskia Tourist Park is an extremely wonderful tourist park, pet friendly, and very well maintained and clean. Very peaceful. With close location to a variety of shops. Excellent choice of a variety of different types of accommodation. Highly recommend.
Amazing maintenance and cleanliness lots of facilities for families to enjoy. And bandicoots☺️

I got a lux pet friendly cabin for two nights. it was magnificently clean!
the fly screen was clean on the rails, no rattle.
the door locks click and also clean on the rails
the windows also clean on the rails and clicked
aircon heater was fast to heat the room.
shower was a nice size and plenty of room in bathroom
the place is quiet and everyone nice.
Close to fast food if you need

wifi was terrible and my code didn't work.
fan rattles a bit in the bathroom

i have them 5 stars though.... why?

because i left my mobile phone behind in the room after leaving and it got returned.
you can the after hours number and the guy picks up or calls back.

I'll be going back for sure!

thanks Banksia!
Very friendly staff

Amenities and cabins are cold and basic. Have been text after hours suggesting im breaking park rules when im unloading belongings out of my car into a cabin in the pouring rain. (There was plenty of room for a car to pass,  I was there 15 mins)

The laundry equipment leaves something to be desired, I used to drive to Midland to do my washing.

I have been insulted by staff at reception an example below.

After spending 3 days in their superior pet friendly cabin then transferring across to a powered site on the 4th day. I forgot to take my keycard with me. Needless to say I had already paid online for my accomodation. Anyway, I apologised as soon as I realised and said please don't make me walk all the way back to get it.

I will add I had been frequenting the park for the past year and they already have my card details.

The girl at the desk stood up, straightened her shirt and stood there like she was a ranking officer of the bloody royal guard. She looked at me and said "So you know Karen, it is the park "Protocol" that we sight your card or you cannot stay here." I will let it go this time only, dont let it happen again!

WELL EXCUSE ME!!!

From what I've experienced personally, what other people have told me and what I can see posted here, nothing will ever change.

I was embarrassed that day and simply didn't have my whits about me. On thinking about this and based on my personal experience working in hospitality I wouldnt dream of speaking to a guest that way.  As for her addressing me by my Christian name, I never gave her that permission.
Needless to say I'm old enough to be her mother. There was a young man sitting behind the desk at the time, it's possible she was out to impress.

Unfortunately it's cost Banksia another customer and im sure after reading other complaints and responses that this will be met with similar responses and excuses.
Unfortunately rude staff regardless of what "Protocol" happens to be, will always drive customers away

Regards
Karen
Super clean, hygienic facilities. Stayed overnight in a motor home. Very friendly and respectful community! Very peaceful!
